  • Parameter Estimation

Parameter Estimation#

BaseModelCalibration is the abstract base class for parameter estimation problems. It handles parameter normalisation (linear or log scale), subset selection, and k-fold splitting; subclasses supply compute_y_sim() and compute_residuals().

SteadyStatePolarizationCurveCalibration is the concrete implementation for fitting to multi-condition polarization and HFR data. It uses scipy.optimize.differential_evolution as the global optimiser and supports:

  • k-fold cross-validation (set_k_folds()) to estimate out-of-sample RMSE for each model complexity.

  • 1-SE rule (optimal_n_1se()) to select the simplest model within one standard error of the best-CV model.

  • Complexity sweep over parameter subsets, accessed via subset_of_unknown_parameters().

Parameters are declared with Parameter (known, fixed value) and UnknownParameter (unknown, optimised; carries bounds and initial guess).

Base class#

class marapendi.estimation.base_calibration.BaseModelCalibration(known_parameters: list, unknown_parameters: list)[source]#

Bases: object

Parameters:

  • known_parameters (list)

  • unknown_parameters (list)

automatic_parameter_selection(n_parameters)[source]#

Select the top-n_parameters parameters by Hessian ranking and move the rest to known.

calculate_local_sensitivity_neighborhood(unknown_p_values, eps_p=0)[source]#

Local sensitivity by finite differences in the normalised parameter space (eq. 7, Goshtasbi et al. 2020).

compute_global_sensitivity(params=None, m=8, check_samples=False, rmse_limit=0.3, print_px=False, filename_to_save=None)[source]#

Global sensitivity via Sobol sampling and local finite-difference sensitivities (eqs. 8–10, Goshtasbi et al. 2020).

Uses 2**m Sobol samples. If check_samples=True, rejects parameter sets whose RMSE exceeds rmse_limit. Results are stored on self (norm_s_i, cosPhi_med_ij, S_med_i, …) and optionally saved to filename_to_save.npz.

compute_residuals(params, case_list=[])[source]#

Return element-wise residuals (y_exp − y_sim) for case_list. Override in subclasses.

compute_y_sim(params, case_list=[])[source]#

Return the concatenated simulated output vector for case_list. Override in subclasses.

estimate(params=None, case_list=None, popsize=10, workers=1, rtol=0, atol=0, ftol=0, method='differential_evolution', initial_guess=None, maxiter=120)[source]#

Minimise mean squared residuals over case_list. Defaults to differential_evolution; pass method=’Nelder-Mead’ etc. for scipy.optimize.minimize.

get_smallest_hessian_eigenvalues()[source]#

Rank parameters by identifiability via QR-pivoted Hessian eigenvalues (Goshtasbi et al. 2020; Lund & Foss 2008).

Stores self.P (parameter ranking by QR pivot) and self.min_eigvals (smallest eigenvalue per subset size). Small eigenvalues indicate collinearity or poor identifiability.

known_parameters: list#
load_cross_validation_results(filename, dir='.')[source]#

Read a previously saved k-fold CV results CSV and return it as a DataFrame.

load_global_sensitivity_results(filename)[source]#

Load sensitivity results previously saved by compute_global_sensitivity.

p_to_theta(unknown_p_values)[source]#

Map physical parameter values to the normalised [0, 1] space (eqs. 5–6, Goshtasbi et al. 2020).

Parameters with is_linear=True use linear scaling; those with is_linear=False use log-scale normalisation.

reset_unknown_parameters()[source]#

Restore all parameters to their original known/unknown split.

run_cross_validation(testing_cases, training_cases=None, estimate_kwargs=None)[source]#

Estimate on training_cases (all cases minus testing_cases by default) and return a results DataFrame.

run_k_fold_cross_validation(k_folds=None, estimate_kwargs=None, filename=None, output_dir='.')[source]#

Run cross-validation for each fold and return a concatenated results DataFrame.

run_k_fold_cross_validation_vs_complexity(n_params_list, force_restart=True, k_folds=None, estimate_kwargs=None, filename=None, output_dir='.')[source]#

Run k-fold CV for each complexity level in n_params_list, checkpointing to CSV after each level.

set_k_folds(k=1)[source]#

Partition self.full_case_list into k folds and store them in self.k_folds.

When k == len(cases) the order is preserved (leave-one-out); otherwise the list is shuffled randomly before splitting.

set_known_params(known_p_list)[source]#

Merge fixed-value parameters from known_p_list into self.params.

set_params(params)[source]#

Replace the full parameter dict with params.

set_unknown_params(unknown_p_list)[source]#

Rebuild all derived arrays and mappings from a list of UnknownParameter objects.

subset_of_unknown_parameters(indices=None, keys=None)[source]#

Promote a subset of unknown parameters to known, keeping only the selected ones unknown.

Parameters:

  • indices (list of int, optional) – Positions in self.unknown_parameters to keep as unknown.

  • keys (list of str, optional) – Alternative to indices — parameter keys to keep as unknown.

theta_to_p(theta_k)[source]#

Map normalised [0, 1] parameters to physical values (eqs. 5–6, Goshtasbi et al. 2020).

unknown_parameters: list#

Polarization curve calibration#

class marapendi.estimation.polarization_curve_calibration.SteadyStatePolarizationCurveCalibration(known_parameters, unknown_parameters, conditions_dataset, experimental_dataset, cell_creator, cell_model=<factory>)[source]#

Bases: BaseModelCalibration

Calibration of a steady-state polarisation curve model against voltage and HFR data.

conditions_dataset : per-case operating conditions (temperature, pressures, RH, stoichiometries). experimental_dataset : per-case measurements with columns case, current-density, voltage, hfr. cell_creator : callable(params) → FuelCell used to build the model from a parameter dict. cell_model : solver instance; defaults to ExplicitSteadyStateModel.

Parameters:

  • known_parameters (list)

  • unknown_parameters (list)

  • conditions_dataset (DataFrame)

  • experimental_dataset (DataFrame)

  • cell_creator (Callable)

  • cell_model (ExplicitSteadyStateModel)

apply_hfr_weights(hfr)[source]#

Scale HFR values so their residuals are comparable in magnitude to voltage residuals.

Multiplies by 1e4 (unit conversion from Ω·m² to mΩ·cm²) and by hfr_weight_factor (ratio of voltage to HFR measurement counts).

build_cases_conditions(current_density=None)[source]#

Build and store a CellConditions object for every case in full_case_list.

build_cell_from_unknown_p_vector(unknown_p_vector)[source]#

Build a FuelCell from the current known params merged with unknown_p_vector.

build_y_exp(case)[source]#

Return the concatenated [voltage | weighted HFR] experimental vector for case.

build_y_exp_cases(case_list)[source]#

Concatenate experimental output vectors for all cases in case_list.

build_y_sim(cell, case)[source]#

Return the concatenated [voltage | weighted HFR] simulated vector for case.

build_y_sim_cases(cell, case_list)[source]#

Concatenate simulated output vectors for all cases in case_list.

cell_creator: Callable#
cell_model: ExplicitSteadyStateModel#
compute_residuals(unknown_p_vector=None, cell=None, case_list=[])[source]#

Return element-wise residuals (y_exp − y_sim) for case_list.

compute_y_sim(unknown_p_vector=None, cell=None, case_list=[])[source]#

Return the concatenated simulated output vector for all cases.

Either unknown_p_vector (physical values) or cell must be provided. When case_list is empty, all cases in self.full_case_list are used.

conditions_dataset: DataFrame#
experimental_dataset: DataFrame#
get_case_dataset(case)[source]#

Return the subset of experimental_dataset for case.

get_estimated_parameters_from_fold_results(fold_results)[source]#

Extract the estimated-parameter columns from a fold-results DataFrame row.

make_conditions(case, current_density=None)[source]#

Build CellConditions for a case. If current_density is given, all tabulated columns are interpolated onto it.

populate_exp_dataset_conditions()[source]#

Merge per-case conditions into experimental_dataset and shift current-density by +1 A/m² to avoid division-by-zero at OCV.

simulate_for_fold_results(fold_results)[source]#

Build a cell from fold_results and simulate all cases; return voltage, HFR, and state dicts.

simulate_voltage_and_hfr(cell, case)[source]#

Solve case and return (cell_voltage, hfr, state).

solve_case(cell, case)[source]#

Run the steady-state solver for case and return the final CellState.

marapendi.estimation.polarization_curve_calibration.optimal_n_1se(test_mean, test_std)[source]#

Return the simplest complexity whose mean test RMSE is within 1 SE of the minimum (1-SE rule).

Reference: Hastie, T. et al. “The Elements of Statistical Learning”, §7.3.

marapendi.estimation.polarization_curve_calibration.build_rmse_stats_df(rmse_vs_complexity_df)[source]#

Aggregate per-case RMSE values into summary statistics by complexity level.

Returns a DataFrame indexed by n_params with one column per fold and aggregate columns (Min., Max., Median, Mean).

Parameter declarations#

class marapendi.estimation.parameters.Parameter(value, key=None, symbol=None, units='n.d.', factor=1)[source]#

Bases: object

A fixed model parameter with display metadata.

Parameters:

  • value (float)

  • key (str)

  • symbol (str)

  • units (str)

  • factor (float)

factor: float = 1#
key: str = None#
symbol: str = None#
units: str = 'n.d.'#
value: float#
class marapendi.estimation.parameters.UnknownParameter(value, key=None, symbol=None, units='n.d.', factor=1, initial_guess=None, lower_bound=None, upper_bound=None, is_linear=True)[source]#

Bases: Parameter

A parameter to be estimated, with bounds and normalisation type.

Parameters:

  • value (float)

  • key (str)

  • symbol (str)

  • units (str)

  • factor (float)

  • initial_guess (float)

  • lower_bound (float)

  • upper_bound (float)

  • is_linear (bool)

initial_guess: float = None#
is_linear: bool = True#
lower_bound: float = None#
upper_bound: float = None#
